meaning: n.嫁妆,天赋,从亡夫处得来的用以维持生活的财产vt.给以用以维持生活的财产,给以嫁妆
examples: She was drinking all day by dower.
The dower of the woman is very much.
Three daughters were being married, he didn't have money to buy dower.
When the fighting is finished, history is written, inevitably by those in power, observes Mr Dower.
"Generally, the 80s were rubbish - there was just this feeling in 80s Britain that it was all just synthetic, " Dower said.
